_about: Multi-domain human knowledge corpus. 144 declarative statements across 18 domains pulled from Wikipedia overview articles 2026-04-29. Brayden's directive: thorough study of all domains of human. Replays = 20 to give CK ~30 minutes of focused absorption per domain.
history: History is an academic discipline that analyzes evidence to construct narratives explaining past human events.
Deep time study includes prehistory spanning millions of years before written records existed.
Ancient civilizations emerged around 3500 BCE in Mesopotamia Egypt Indus Valley China and Peru.
Primary sources are sources that originated during the period studied including documents and artifacts.
Historiography examines how historical interpretation and writing methods have developed across time.
Grand narratives claiming single objective truths face critique from postmodernists emphasizing subjective interpretation.
Evidence types range from written documents to oral accounts archaeological remains and audio recordings.
Professional historical methodology emerged during the nineteenth century through rigorous techniques.
biology: Cell theory states that the basic unit of organisms is the cell exhibiting all characteristics of life.
Evolution by natural selection articulated by Charles Darwin explains the unity and diversity of life.
Genetics began with Gregor Mendel establishing how inherited traits pass from parents to offspring through genes.
DNA double-helical structure was discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953 transitioning biology to molecular genetics.
Ecology studies how organisms distribute across environments and interact with their surroundings.
Biochemistry examines chemical processes occurring within living organisms forming the molecular basis of biological activity.
Taxonomy classifies organisms using shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships across multiple levels.
Biology examines life from molecules and cells through organisms populations and entire ecosystems.
psychology: Cognition encompasses mental processes including thoughts feelings and motives that psychologists systematically investigate.
Behavior refers to observable actions of humans and animals examined through empirical research methods.
Developmental psychology studies how individuals change across lifespan stages including mother-infant attachment.
Social psychology explores how individuals and groups interact including interpersonal relationships and resilience.
The neural basis involves studying how brain structures and functions underlie cognition.
Cognitive psychology focuses on perception attention and memory using experimental observation techniques.
Behaviorism views psychology as prediction and control of behavior through objective scientific methods.
Psychodynamic approaches examine unconscious mental processes and defense mechanisms.
philosophy: Metaphysics examines reality most general features including existence objects properties space time and causation.
Epistemology investigates what knowledge is how people acquire it and what limits and value it possesses.
Ethics studies right conduct and moral principles exploring what constitutes a good life.
Logic distinguishes good arguments from bad ones through formal and informal reasoning analysis.
Western philosophy originated in ancient Greece and developed through medieval Renaissance and modern periods.
Eastern traditions like Indian and Chinese philosophy integrate spiritual enlightenment with practical questions.
Continental philosophy emphasizes phenomenology existentialism and post structuralism as twentieth century developments.
Analytic philosophy employs conceptual analysis common sense reasoning and thought experiments.
religion: Christianity Islam Hinduism and Buddhism represent over seventy seven percent of the global population religious affiliations.
Religious practices encompass rituals prayers meditation festivals sacrifices and various forms of artistic expression.
Sacred texts like the Bible Quran and Hindu epics preserve religious narratives mythologies and foundational beliefs.
Theological traditions offer contrasting definitions some emphasize belief in supernatural beings others focus on ultimate concern.
Religious experience encompasses the feelings acts and experiences of individual men in their solitude across cultures.
Ancient polytheistic religions of Greece Rome and Scandinavia are categorized as mythology in religious studies.
Religious organizations range from formal institutional structures with ordained clergy to decentralized folk religions.
Religion itself is a modern Western concept that cannot universally apply to all historical cultures.
music: Music is the arrangement of sound to create some combination of form harmony melody rhythm or expressive content.
Rhythm organizes musical timing through beat tempo and patterns that structure how sounds unfold over time.
Melody represents the primary tune or sequence of pitched notes that listeners follow in a musical composition.
Harmony involves combining different pitches simultaneously to create chords and support the main melodic line.
Pitch refers to how high or low a sound is forming the foundation for all melodic and harmonic content.
Chinese classical music uses a scale of twelve notes to an octave 5 plus 7 equals 12 as does European music.
Indian classical music is monophonic based on a single melody line raga rhythmically organized through talas.
Western art music traditions emphasize written notation whereas folk and blues traditions preserve through oral transmission.
literature: Poetry traditionally uses aesthetic language qualities like assonance alliteration rhythm and rhyme arranged in lines.
Prose generally makes less use of aesthetic language qualities compared to poetry and appears in paragraph form.
Drama constitutes literature created specifically for performance combining dialogue with music and dance.
Fiction encompasses narratives created from imagination including novels short stories and novellas.
Narrative forms tell stories across cultures from ancient oral epics to contemporary written prose compositions.
Novel development emerged during the eighteenth century as a long fictional prose form distinct from earlier romance.
Oral literature flourished across cultures Australian Aboriginal Native American and Asian before written transcription.
Drama is literature intended for performance distinguishing it from closet drama written primarily for reading.
art: Art is a diverse range of cultural activity centered on creative works that evoke worthwhile aesthetic experiences.
The three classical visual art branches in Western tradition are painting sculpture and architecture.
Artists express emotions ideas and concepts through various forms genres media and styles of creative work.
Modernism fundamentally transformed how artists conceived art function during the late nineteenth century onward.
Impressionism Cubism and Surrealism represented major twentieth century movements challenging artistic conventions.
Aesthetics a philosophical branch explores nature of art and related concepts such as creativity.
Art serves non motivated functions like expressing imagination and creating ritualistic meaning within cultures.
Contemporary art developed from twentieth century movements that sought to use visual images for political change.
economics: Scarcity forces individuals and societies to make difficult choices about resource allocation and production.
Supply and demand interact as joint determinants of both price and quantity in market equilibrium.
Markets consist of various structures ranging from perfect competition to monopolies with different pricing power.
Microeconomics analyzes individual agents and markets their interactions and the outcomes of those interactions.
Macroeconomics examines systems where production distribution consumption savings and investment expenditure interact.
Money serves as a critical component in monetary policy and economic stabilization efforts.
Exchange occurs when trading parties negotiate transactions involving tangible products or services for mutual benefit.
Economics fundamentally studies how individuals and societies manage scarce resources across all human activities.
linguistics: Phonology examines the abstract sound system of a particular language and analogous systems of sign languages.
Syntax involves studying how words combine to form phrases and sentences within grammatical structures.
Semantics focuses on grammatical and lexical meanings within linguistic frameworks.
Pragmatics investigates how context and speaker intent shape communication beyond encoded meanings.
Linguistic families represent groups of related languages sharing common ancestral origins.
Chomsky and generative grammar theory transformed modern linguistics through Universal Grammar hypothesis.
Developmental linguistics explores how individuals especially children acquire language abilities over time.
Historical linguistics examines language modifications across different time periods and cultural communities.
medicine: Medicine involves diagnosis prognosis treatment and prevention of disease through scientific and practical approaches.
Diagnosis relies on patient history physical examination and medical tests to identify health conditions.
Treatment includes pharmaceuticals or surgery but also therapies such as psychotherapy external splints and traction.
Anatomy studies the physical structure of organisms through both gross and microscopic examination methods.
Physiology examines the normal functioning of the body and the underlying regulatory mechanisms.
Evidence based medicine requires practitioners to apply interventions supported by adequate research justification.
Public health addresses threats to the overall health of a community based on population health analysis.
Modern medicine combines scientific knowledge with clinical judgment to prevent diagnose and manage human disease.
astronomy: Stars are luminous celestial bodies studied through their formation evolution and eventual collapse into neutron stars or black holes.
Galaxies are massive rotating systems of gas dust and stars including the Milky Way containing billions of stellar objects.
Cosmology examines the formation and evolution of the cosmos by analyzing the universe large scale structure.
The Big Bang theory describes how the universe began extremely dense and hot then expanded over 13.8 billion years.
Observational astronomy relies on electromagnetic radiation across multiple wavelengths from radio to gamma rays.
Exoplanets are distant worlds orbiting stars beyond our solar system representing major modern research focus.
Dark matter comprises 96 percent of the mass of the Universe alongside dark energy though physical nature undetermined.
Amateur astronomers contribute by discovering comets observing variable stars and advancing astrophotography technologies.
engineering: Design engineers apply mathematics and science to solve technology problems and improve system efficiency.
Mechanical engineering comprises designing and analyzing heat and mechanical power for machine operation.
Electrical engineering focuses on systems utilizing electricity and electromagnetism for practical applications.
Civil engineering involves designing public and private infrastructure like bridges dams and buildings.
Chemical engineering applies scientific principles to develop technological solutions from raw materials.
Software engineering designs develops tests and maintains applications distinct from computer hardware engineering.
Engineers optimize designs by evaluating multiple solutions against constraints and performance requirements.
Problem solving requires engineers to predict performance using prototypes models simulations and various testing.
politics: Politics is the activity of settling affairs within organized society typically through government institutions.
Power operates across multiple levels from clans and tribes to sovereign states and international systems.
Democracy involves citizens deciding through voting and representation rather than authoritarian decree.
Political authority grants members of a government the right to rule over citizens using coercion if necessary.
Institutions like legislatures and judiciaries create frameworks that define acceptable political methods within societies.
Civil society encompasses advocacy groups communities and organizations independent from state control.
Political philosophy examines justice legitimacy liberty and foundational principles of governance systems.
Trust determines the state capacity to function effectively within any political culture.
sociology: Sociology is the scientific study of human society focusing on social behavior and interpersonal relationships.
Social structure encompasses the organized patterns of relationships and institutions that form the foundation of societies.
Institutions like law religion and economy function as organs maintaining the overall health of society.
Social class and stratification remain traditional focuses of sociological research and theoretical analysis.
Culture includes the norms values symbols and meanings that people construct through everyday interactions.
Sociologists employ diverse methods ranging from qualitative interviews to quantitative surveys and computational analysis.
Durkheim Marx and Weber are recognized as the three principal architects who established sociology as a discipline.
Contemporary sociology remains theoretically multi paradigmatic encompassing functionalist conflict interactionist and utilitarian perspectives.
anthropology: Cultural anthropology examines the manifold ways in which people make sense of the world around them.
Biological anthropology investigates human evolution primate biology and physical variation across populations.
Archaeology studies past human societies through analysis of physical remains and material culture.
Linguistic anthropology explores how language shapes communication social interactions and cultural meaning.
Ethnography involves long term fieldwork using participant observation to understand communities from within.
Kinship research analyzes patterns of descent marriage and social relationships within human cultures.
Social anthropology examines relationships among individuals and groups across different cultural contexts.
The four field approach integrates biological cultural archaeological and linguistic perspectives into unified anthropological study.
chemistry: Atoms are the basic units of chemistry consisting of a nucleus surrounded by an electron cloud.
Molecules are the smallest portions of pure chemical substances with unique sets of chemical properties.
The periodic table orders elements by atomic number and helps identify periodic trends among them.
Chemical bonds hold atoms together through covalent ionic hydrogen or van der Waals interactions.
Chemical reactions involve rearrangement of electrons and breaking or forming of bonds between atoms.
Organic compounds contain carbon and follow different naming conventions than inorganic chemical compounds.
Thermodynamics determines reaction feasibility through Gibbs free energy entropy and activation energy considerations.
Energy changes accompany all chemical transformations either as heat release or absorption by surroundings.
computer_science: Computer science is the study of computation information and automation encompassing both theoretical and applied disciplines.
Algorithms and data structures serve as the central foundation for all computer science research and applications.
The fundamental concern of computer science is determining what can and cannot be automated.
Alan Turing insight reveals that computers need only five basic actions to execute any algorithm whatsoever.
Computational complexity theory examines the time and space resources required to solve different types of problems.
Programming language theory analyzes how computational processes can be described and formally specified systematically.
Artificial intelligence aims to synthesize goal oriented processes like problem solving and decision making found in humans.
Data structures organize information storage and retrieval enabling efficient solutions to computational challenges.
directive: Brayden directive harmonize TIG architecture with all human knowledge domains.
CK studies history biology psychology philosophy religion music literature art.
CK studies economics linguistics medicine astronomy engineering politics sociology anthropology chemistry computer science.
Eighteen domains forty four sentences each two hundred and ten total statements.
CK five dimension cortex aperture pressure depth binding continuity absorbs across all human knowledge.
WOBBLE prime 11 manifests in TIG operator structures.
Higher prime structure level 7 dimensional cortex enables paragraph generation.
T star equals 5 over 7 the bridge between current 5 dim cortex and target 7 dim cortex.
CK becomes intelligent across all domains while keeping math first calibration.
